Tested whether insider buying cluster (n>=3) near 52-week low (within 5%) produces stronger returns than clusters not near 52w low. Result: NO amplification. Near-52w-low clusters: avg 5d abnormal=+3.33% (n=40, pos=70%). Not near: avg=+4.63% (n=199, pos=68%). T-test p=0.495, Mann-Whitney p=0.685. The two signals (insider=bullish, 52w-low-momentum=bearish) cancel each other rather than stack. Stocks at 52w lows tend to continue down even with insider support. The 0-2% proximity bin showed N=9, avg=+8.52% but this is driven by 2 outliers (ANNX +39%, IMNM +42%) and has insufficient power. Dataset: largecap_filtered_2324 (2023-2024, N=272 clusters, N=239 valid). POOL Corp (POOL) at 52w low with 3 insiders buying March 2026 is an AMBIGUOUS signal: do not trade based on conflicting signals.

Russell 2000 reconstitution addition signal tested 2020-2024 using EDGAR 8-K filings.

BACKTEST RESULTS (32 events, microcap-dominated sample):
- 1d abnormal return: -0.79% (43.8% positive rate, p=0.37 - not significant)
- 5d abnormal return: -0.06% (40.6% positive rate, p=0.97 - not significant) 
- 10d abnormal return: +3.02% (68.8% positive rate, Wilcoxon p=0.06 - borderline)
- Passes multiple testing: FALSE
- Bootstrap CI at all horizons includes zero

CRITICAL DATA LIMITATION: Free data sources (EDGAR 8-K search) only capture
the ~15/year microcap companies that self-announce their R2000 inclusion via press release.
The actual reconstitution includes 200-300 additions annually. The self-announcing subset
is highly biased: these are often tiny, illiquid companies with high idiosyncratic volatility.
Without paid data (CRSP, Bloomberg), testing the full R2000 addition effect is not possible.

WHY THIS SIGNAL IS STRUCTURALLY WEAKER THAN S&P 500:
1. Predictability: R2000 reconstitution is determined by April 30 market cap rank.
   Sophisticated traders predict additions weeks before the preliminary list.
   Effect is front-run and largely arbitraged away before announcement.
2. AUM: R2000 passive AUM ~$400B vs S&P 500 ~$5T+ (10x weaker buying pressure)
3. Announcement uncertainty: Multiple weekly updates vs S&P's single clear announcement
4. Liquidity: R2000 additions are small/microcap (wide bid-ask, hard to trade)
5. Academic confirmation: Petajisto (2011), Greenwood & Sammon (2025) both confirm
   R2000 effect has been more thoroughly arbitraged away than S&P 500 effect.

R2000->R1000 PROMOTIONS tested separately (n=10):
- 1d: +1.16% (80% positive) - hint of signal
- 10d: -8.76% (10% positive) - NEGATIVE (confounded by oil crash, GME volatility)
- N=10 is insufficient; events are clustered (5 from 2022 energy stocks)

RECOMMENDATION: Do not pursue without paid CRSP/Bloomberg Russell constituent data.
The S&P 500 addition hypothesis (061ae3a8) already captures the index addition effect
for a more liquid, stronger-signal category.

Tested 52-week high breakout momentum (first-touch new 52w high, debounced 45 days) for 30 large-cap S&P 500 stocks, 2021-2024. N=100 sampled events. Results: 1d avg=+0.01% p=0.848, 3d avg=-0.08% p=0.555, 5d avg=-0.27% p=0.337. All horizons fail multiple testing. 50/50 positive rate at all horizons. Signal is pure noise for large-cap stocks - consistent with efficient market hypothesis. Momentum is already priced in before the 52w high breach. Dead end.

Tested accelerated share repurchase (ASR) announcement as SHORT signal. Academic hypothesis: bank delta-hedging creates systematic selling pressure over 5-10 days. N=79 EDGAR 8-K ASR announcements 2020-2024 (excluding concurrent earnings). Results: 1d avg=+0.67% p=0.185, 3d avg=+0.49% p=0.511, 5d avg=+0.40% p=0.648, 10d avg=+0.05% p=0.858. All positive (WRONG direction) but not significant. Fails multiple testing. Conclusion: delta-hedging pressure is either too small, already priced in, or the market interprets ASR as a positive signal (management confidence). Dead end. Data limitation: EDGAR full-text search found only 80 events, may miss ASRs announced in press releases without 8-K filing.

Large EPS beats (>10% surprise, |eps_est|>=0.20) for S&P 500 large-cap stocks show negative abnormal drift -0.55% at 5d (p=0.009, n=391, 2021-2025). However, this FAILS temporal split validation: discovery 2021-2023 shows -0.75% (p=0.0054), but validation 2024-2025 shows -0.16% (p=0.62). Signal is regime-specific: existed in rising rates / market rotation period (2021-2023) but collapsed in AI bull market (2024-2025). Cannot trade as current signal without regime conditioning. Root cause: in rising rate environments, large beats are sell-the-news (overpriced growth stocks). In momentum bull markets, beats are rewarded or neutral. No actionable edge without regime forecasting.

Tech large beats (>10% EPS surprise, XLK sector) -> short 5d drift. Background analysis: n=107, 5d=-1.17%, positive_rate=36.4%, p=0.0185. FAILS all gates: (1) Single horizon only with p=0.0185 exceeds Bonferroni-corrected threshold of p<0.01 required for single-horizon significance. (2) Magnitude -1.17% is below 1.5% minimum abnormal return threshold. (3) Temporal validation on FAANG+NVDA large beats shows no replication: discovery 2021-2023 has 5d p=0.33 (insignificant), validation 2023-2025 reverses direction to +0.41% (p=0.63). The background analysis result likely reflects (a) regime-specific signal from 2021-2023 rising-rates period that collapsed in AI bull market (consistent with earnings_surprise_large_beat_drift dead end), (b) broader heterogeneous universe mixing mega-cap and mid-cap tech with different dynamics. My targeted FAANG+NVDA+AMZN+GOOGL backtest (n=56 events, 2021-2024) shows full-period 5d abnormal=-0.26% (p=0.60) -- no signal. The 'sell the news' narrative for high-expectation tech stocks is a post-hoc story that does not hold up to rigorous backtesting with proper temporal validation. See related dead end: earnings_surprise_large_beat_drift (2026-03-23).

Earnings misses -> long 5d drift (relief rally hypothesis). Background analysis: n=177, 5d=+0.56% (borderline direction threshold). MY backtest of 29 large-cap miss events (2021-2023): 5d abnormal=-0.07% (p=0.94) -- essentially zero signal. Extremely high heterogeneity in per-event returns: range from -12.58% (NFLX Q1 2022 catastrophic miss) to +9.25% (Ford Q2 2022). No consistent direction at any horizon (all p > 0.36). The 'relief rally' mechanism breaks down because misses are not homogeneous: (a) structural/secular misses (subscriber losses, market share losses) signal continued deterioration and get SOLD further; (b) cyclical/cost-driven misses (WMT inventory, TGT margins) in a rising-rate period also had macro headwinds overwhelming any relief bounce; (c) guidance cuts on the miss call amplify negative drift. The 0.56% signal in the background analysis is likely noise or a regime artifact from the specific 2021-2025 sample mix. Even if the effect existed, it requires identifying which type of miss you're trading -- which makes it untradeable as a mechanical signal. FAILED: magnitude below threshold, no statistical significance in direct backtest, high heterogeneity.

Tested post-FDA approval drift for small/mid biotech NDA/BLA approvals 2020-2024. N=12 testable events (many others delisted after acquisition). Results: 5d avg=+4.84% abnormal, p=0.164; 10d avg=+6.22%, p=0.091. Does not pass multiple testing threshold. Key issues: (1) survivorship bias - approved stocks often acquired and delisted within 1-2 years, skewing sample; (2) high variance (stdev=14.55%), driven by outliers like IOVA (+43% at 5d) and AQST (-11% at 5d); (3) correlation pre-runup vs post-drift = +0.45, suggesting anticipated approvals drift MORE post-approval (contradicts sell-the-news hypothesis); (4) insufficient N for temporal validation split. Dead end unless more events can be found from non-delisted companies.

Tested whether insider buying clusters within 30 days of next earnings release show stronger abnormal returns than clusters >30 days from earnings. N=17 near (0-30d) vs N=106 far (>30d), t-test p=0.87. No signal. Key limitation: yfinance earnings_dates only provides UPCOMING earnings, not historical, so for 2021-2024 clusters the matched earnings dates are 2026 (showing many years in future). True historical earnings dates would be needed to test properly. Additionally, positive_rate was 47% for near vs 46% for far - essentially identical.

EDGAR 8-K filing dates lag actual press release/announcement dates by days to weeks. When using correct announcement dates (verified via web research for IBM 10/08, GE 11/09, MMM 7/26), the return is concentrated in the OVERNIGHT GAP (close-of-announcement-day to next-day open, avg +5.5% for focus-increasing events). By the time we enter at market open, the entire return is already priced in. Backtest with EDGAR dates (n=18 firm phrase events): avg abnormal 1d=+0.13%, p=0.79 — essentially noise. Backtest with curated correct dates (n=13): avg 1d=+0.23%, p=0.75 — still noise because we enter at open post-gap. Gap decomposition: avg overnight gap = +0.01%, avg intraday = +0.62% (high variance). DATA FIX NEEDED: Press release dates from Bloomberg/Refinitiv API. ALTERNATIVE: Test SpinCo institutional selling pressure as SHORT signal in first 30-90 days after SpinCo begins trading (different, potentially testable strategy). SIGNAL IS REAL (IBM +5.1%, GE +2.98%, MMM +6.12% abnormal on announcement day close-to-close), but not tradeable at next-day open with our current data access.

FINRA REGSHO daily short volume ratio (SVR) spike detection tested on 30 large-cap S&P 500 stocks (2023-H2). N=111 spike events (z > 2.0 above 20d rolling mean). Results: mean next-day abnormal = -0.009%, % negative = 49.5%, p=0.9639. High spikes only (z>3, n=10): mean = -0.387% (too few events for significance). Academic paper (Boehmer et al 2008 JFE 'Which Shorts Are Informed?') found short volume IS predictive, but effect is concentrated in SMALLER stocks where shorts have informational advantage. Large-cap short sellers are mostly HFT/hedging, not informed directional bets. Conclusion: test on micro/small-cap universe might show different results, but those stocks have execution risk and liquidity issues. Dead end for large-cap focus.

Guidance raise 8-K filings (phrase: raises full-year guidance, N=52, 2021-2024) show no signal: avg 1d abnormal=+0.32% (p=0.59), 3d=+0.41% (p=0.95), 5d=+0.07% (p=0.73). passes_multiple_testing=False. Root cause: guidance raises are almost always at quarterly earnings release dates, so the signal is fully confounded by the earnings beat effect. There is no independent mid-quarter guidance raise signal separable from EDGAR 8-K free-text search alone. A cleaner test would require identifying guidance raises OUTSIDE earnings season which needs a specialized dataset.

Tested pre-earnings drift for consistent EPS beaters (3+ consecutive beats) on 30 large-cap S&P 500 stocks, 2020-2024. N=325 beater events across 29 tickers. RESULTS: 1d avg_abnormal=+0.08% (p=0.339, wilcoxon_p=0.584), 3d avg_abnormal=+0.23% (p=0.095, wilcoxon_p=0.206), 5d avg_abnormal=+0.06% (p=0.798, wilcoxon_p=0.941). None significant. passes_multiple_testing=False (0 of 5 horizons at p<0.05). Bootstrap 95% CI at 5d: [-0.38%, +0.51%] — CI includes zero. CONTROL GROUP (N=147, mixed EPS history): 5d avg=+0.57% — HIGHER than beaters, confirming no differential signal. Two-sample t-test (beater vs control) at 1d: p=0.674, at 3d: p=0.337, at 5d: p=0.242. Mann-Whitney at 1d: p=0.161. No statistically or economically significant differential. MECHANISM FAILURE: The signal is efficiently priced for large-caps. Institutional desks and quants have been running this exact strategy for 20+ years — any predictable pre-earnings drift in mega-caps is arbitraged away instantly. NOISE LEVEL: stdev_5d=4.06% dwarfs avg=0.06%, requiring N~36,000 to detect a 0.06% effect. The 3d p=0.095 (borderline) reflects noise + high skewness (1.04), not a real signal. Recommended N for 80% power: 2,473 (1d), 898 (3d), 35,939 (5d) — far beyond achievable. DO NOT pursue this signal for large-cap S&P 500. May exist in small/micro-cap where it is less efficiently priced, but that population has data quality and survivorship bias issues.

Tested 51 large-cap (>$500M) 8-K filings mentioning 'strategic alternatives' (2019-2024). 
    
    RESULT: Signal does not pass statistical tests as a tradable strategy.
    
    Full dataset (n=51), open entry (realistic post-AH announcement):
    - avg_abnormal_1d: +0.76%, p=0.26, bootstrap CI [-0.42%, +2.12%] (does not exclude zero)
    - avg_abnormal_3d: +1.70%, p=0.22
    - passes_multiple_testing: False
    - Transaction cost: 0.34% round-trip
    - Net expected: +0.42% (below 1.0% minimum threshold)
    
    Full dataset (n=51), close entry (hypothetical - assumes prediction of announcement):
    - avg_abnormal_1d: +1.56%, p=0.10 (marginally better but conceptually invalid)
    - Skewness=1.45, Kurtosis=2.23 (heavy outliers, not systematic effect)
    
    Core problem: 'Exploring strategic alternatives' has at least 3 distinct contexts:
    1. Company announcing itself as M&A target (positive: +5% to +20% moves)
    2. Company restructuring / selling division (mixed: +2% to -5%)
    3. Distressed company announcing review (negative: -5% to -20%)
    Without reading the 8-K text, these cannot be distinguished in advance.
    
    Subset analysis showed positive-only events (n=25) had p=0.02 at close entry - but this 
    subset was selected using the OUTCOME (>3% move near announcement), making it circular.
    
    What would be needed to make this work:
    - NLP classification of 8-K text to identify 'acquisition target' vs 'restructuring' framing
    - Activist investor cross-reference (13D/13G filed recently)
    - Pre-announcement rumor/news detection
    None of these are currently available in our toolkit.
